title: A Lament
content:
The merry merry lark was up and singing,
         And the hare was out and feeding on the lea;
    And the merry merry bells below were ringing,
         When my child's laugh rang through me.

    Now the hare is snared and dead beside the snow-yard,
         And the lark beside the dreary winter sea;
    And the baby in his cradle in the churchyard
         Sleeps sound till the bell brings me.

    Eversley, 1848.
meaning:
"A Lament" is a quintessential example of Charles Kingsley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
